page.title: Formal Register and Nominalisation Basics
page.subtitle_html: Formal Register and Nominalisation Basics - B2 formal wording and basic noun-based alternatives.
page.subtitle_text: B2 formal wording and basic noun-based alternatives.

blocks.body: {"level":"B2","intro":"Formal Register and Nominalisation Basics: compact B2 anchors for formal verbs, noun phrases, and nominalisation.","rules":[{"label":"Formal verbs","text":"Use conduct, obtain, require, and indicate instead of more casual alternatives when needed.","example":"The team conducted a review."},{"label":"Nominalisation","text":"Turn actions into noun phrases for formal style.","example":"The improvement of the system is important."},{"label":"Register control","text":"Choose neutral or formal wording for reports, essays, and professional messages.","example":"Further information is required."}]}

blocks.heading: B2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English B2 lesson package.
